pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 40, 49, 80 and export_result.sh line 32 - hardcoded collection name consistently used
task.json description, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh lines 35, 44, 46, and verifier.py lines 93-95, 100-102 - hardcoded consistently
verifier.py scoring logic: new_name_exists (30) + old_name_removed (20) + id_preservation (20) + item_preservation (20) = 90 points total for database checks
verifier.py lines 138, 148, 151 - VLM check grants maximum 10 points when collection rename is visually confirmed in UI

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on Jurism (Juris-M), a real legal reference manager software based on Zotero, specifically designed for legal research. The task injects real US Supreme Court cases (Brown v. Board of Education, Marbury v. Madison, Miranda v. Arizona, New York Times Co. v. Sullivan, Gideon v. Wainwright, Obergefell v. Hodges, Tinker v. Des Moines) and real law review articles from Harvard Law Review, Columbia Law Review, and Yale Law Journal into a Jurism database. The task then asks the user to rename a collection from 'Research' to 'First Amendment Jurisprudence' - both legitimate collection names for legal research. Verification uses database integrity checks (90 points maximum for: new name exists, old name removed, collection ID preserved, items preserved) plus VLM visual confirmation (10 points). The total scoring scheme: 90 points from database checks + 10 points from VLM = 100 points maximum.
